items.text:
Subject: Campus Radio Station — Volunteer Recruitment

Hi Briar,

I'm writing to invite you to volunteer at WCRX, the student-run campus radio station, for the spring semester. We are recruiting fifteen new volunteers across three teams: on-air hosts, technical operators, and weekend producers. Each volunteer commits to a minimum of three hours per week starting March 1, and all training takes place during the last week of February in Lockwood Hall, Studio B.

A $25 station fee covers your access card, headphone rental, and the volunteer T-shirt. Returning volunteers who served last semester pay only $10. If you're interested in joining the team, please complete the application form on our website by February 18 and send a brief audio sample to Mr. Patel at the station office. Selected volunteers will be notified by February 22 at the latest.

Let me know if you have any questions before the deadline.

Best,
Lena Marquez
Station Manager, WCRX

questions.stem: According to the email, where does volunteer training take place?
questions.explanation: The email states that all training takes place during the last week of February in Lockwood Hall, Studio B.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a returning volunteer's costs?

options.A: Returning volunteers receive a complimentary new T-shirt.
options.B: Returning volunteers pay less than first-time volunteers do.
options.C: Returning volunteers must purchase additional studio equipment.
options.D: Returning volunteers are required to attend extra training sessions.

questions.explanation: The email indicates that returning volunteers pay only $10 while new volunteers pay $25, so returning volunteers pay less.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the email?

options.A: To remind volunteers of an upcoming weekend show
options.B: To recruit new volunteers and explain the application process
options.C: To announce a change in station management for the semester
options.D: To request a donation for new studio equipment

questions.explanation: The email's main purpose is to invite the recipient to join as a volunteer, explain the teams and training, and describe how to apply.

items.text:
Subject: Flu Shot Appointment Confirmation

Dear Theo,

This email confirms your flu shot appointment at the Westgate Health Clinic on Tuesday, October 17 at 11:15 AM. Please arrive at the second-floor reception desk in Westgate Hall ten minutes before your scheduled time and bring your student ID and insurance card.

The nurse on duty will be Ms. Yang. After the injection, you will be asked to remain in the waiting area for fifteen minutes so that staff can monitor for any unusual reactions. The standard charge is $0 for students with the campus health plan; visitors and dependents pay a $25 fee on site.

If you need to reschedule, please respond to this email at least twenty-four hours in advance so the slot can be reassigned. Walk-in appointments are not available during the busy first week of November.

Best regards,
Westgate Health Clinic

questions.stem: According to the email, what should Theo bring to the appointment?

options.A: A prescription note from his family physician
options.B: Student identification and an insurance card
options.C: A signed consent form from his academic advisor
options.D: A printed copy of his recent immunization history

questions.explanation: The email asks Theo to bring his student ID and insurance card to the appointment.

questions.stem: According to the email, why must Theo remain in the waiting area after the injection?

options.A: Staff want him to attend a brief wellness presentation.
options.B: Staff need him to complete additional registration paperwork.
options.C: Staff want to observe him for any unusual reactions.
options.D: Staff need him to verify the billing information.

questions.explanation: The email states that he will be asked to remain in the waiting area for fifteen minutes so that staff can monitor for any unusual reactions.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about scheduling near the first week of November?

options.A: The clinic typically closes for staff training that week.
options.B: The clinic generally lowers its fees during November.
options.C: The clinic is unusually busy and cannot accept walk-ins.
options.D: The clinic relocates to a temporary site that week.

questions.explanation: The email notes that walk-in appointments are not available during the busy first week of November, implying that the clinic is unusually busy then.
